Name: Sewaddle
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

Name: Swadloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

Name: Leavanny
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it has no Weakness.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

Name: Petilil
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Since they prefer moist, nutrient-rich soil, the areas where Petilil live are known to be good for growing plants.

Name: Lilligant
Effects: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect. It withers if a Trainer does not take good care of it.

Name: Dwebble
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: The Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth.

Name: Crustle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle.

Name: Karrablast
Effects: If Shelmet is in play, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When they feel threatened, they spit acidic liquid to drive attackers away. This Pokémon targets Shelmet.

Name: Foongus
Flavor-Text: For some reason, this Pokémon resembles a Poké Ball. They release poison spores to repel those who try to catch them.

Name: Amoonguss
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: They show off their Poké Ball caps to lure prey, but very few Pokémon are fooled by this.

Name: Shelmet
Effects: If Karrablast is in play, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It evolves when bathed in an electric-like energy along with Karrablast. The reason is still unknown.

Name: Accelgor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Having removed its heavy shell, it becomes very light and can fight with ninja-like movements.

Name: Virizion
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Leaf Wallop attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents.

Name: Victini
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack, you may ignore all effects of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. You can’t use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.
Effects: Move all Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Victini
Effects: If you have 4 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Pansear
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600° F.

Name: Simisear
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A flame burns inside its body. It scatters embers from its head and tail to sear its opponents.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Before doing damage, discard a Pokémon Tool card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using their very hot, flame-covered tongues, they burn through Durant’s steel bodies and consume their insides.

Name: Larvesta
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

Name: Larvesta
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The base of volcanoes is where they make their homes. They shoot fire from their five horns to repel attacking enemies.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona’s fire provided a replacement for the sun.

Name: Tympole
Flavor-Text: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk.

Name: Palpitoad
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.
Flavor-Text: It lives in water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to capture prey.

Name: Seismitoad
Effects: Does 30 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.
Flavor-Text: It increases the power of its punches by vibrating the bumps on its fists. It can turn a boulder to rubble with one punch.

Name: Tirtouga
Flavor-Text: Restored from a fossil, this Pokémon can dive to depths beyond half a mile.

Name: Carracosta
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, reduce that damage by 50 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They can live both in the ocean and on land. A slap from one of them is enough to open a hole in the bottom of a tanker.

Name: Vanillite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow.

Name: Vanillish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It conceals itself from enemy eyes by creating many small ice particles and hiding among them.

Name: Vanilluxe
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. If either of them is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: If both heads get angry simultaneously, this Pokémon expels a blizzard, burying everything in snow.

Name: Frillish
Flavor-Text: With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor.

Name: Jellicent
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused.
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The fate of ships and crew that wander into Jellicent’s habitats: all sunken, all lost, all vanished.

Name: Cryogonal
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
If the Defending Pokémon is a [F] Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They are born in snow clouds. They use chains made of ice crystals to capture prey.

Name: Cryogonal
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
You may return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Flavor-Text: They are born in snow clouds. They use chains made of ice crystals to capture prey.

Name: Kyurem
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It generates powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

Name: Blitzle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear. It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.

Name: Zebstrika
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard all [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Does 20 more damage if you have any [L] Pokémon on your Bench.
Flavor-Text: They live on treetops and glide using the inside of a cape-like membrane while discharging electricity.

Name: Tynamo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

Name: Tynamo
Flavor-Text: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

Name: Eelektrik
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [L] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

Name: Eelektross
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

Name: Victini
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It creates an unlimited supply of energy inside its body, which it shares with those who touch it.

Name: Yamask
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

Name: Yamask
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves in past ages. Each retains memories of its former life.

Name: Cofagrigus
Effects: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

Name: Cofagrigus
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They pretend to be elaborate coffins to teach lessons to grave robbers. Their bodies are covered in pure gold.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: Put a card from your discard pile on top of your deck.
Flavor-Text: The combination of garbage bags and industrial waste caused the chemical reaction that created this Pokémon.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: They absorb garbage and make it part of their bodies. They shoot poisonous liquid from their right-hand fingertips.

Name: Solosis
Flavor-Text: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

Name: Duosion
Flavor-Text: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

Name: Reuniclus
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Does 40 damage times the number of Reuniclus you have in play.
Flavor-Text: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

Name: Reuniclus
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: These remarkably intelligent Pokémon fight by controlling arms that can grip with rock-crushing power.

Name: Elgyem
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon had never been seen until it appeared from far in the desert 50 years ago.

Name: Elgyem
Effects: Search your deck for 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent’s brain, causing unendurable headaches.

Name: Beheeyem
Effects: Does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that shares a type with the Defending Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It uses psychic power to control an opponent’s brain and tamper with its memories.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel it burns.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leaches off the life force of any who follow it.

Name: Lampent
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

Name: Gigalith
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each [F] Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.

Name: Timburr
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty.

Name: Gurdurr
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

Name: Conkeldurr
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon gets +20 HP for each [F] Energy attached to it.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It it thought that Conkeldurr taught humans how to make concrete more than 2,000 years ago.

Name: Conkeldurr
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: They use concrete pillars as walking canes. They know moves that enable them to swing the pillars freely in battle.

Name: Archen
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another.

Name: Archeops
Ability-Effect: Each play can’t play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve his or her Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a volt of electricity.

Name: Mienfoo
Flavor-Text: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attack. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

Name: Mienshao
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It wield the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arms attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

Name: Golett
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are thought to have been created by the science of an ancient and mysterious civilization.

Name: Golurk
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is said that Golurk were ordered to protect people and Pokémon by the ancient people who made them.

Name: Terrakion
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its charge is strong enough to break through a giant castle wall in one blow. This Pokémon is spoken of in legends.

Name: Landorus
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

Name: Pawniard
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They fight at Bisharp’s command. They cling to their prey and inflict damage by sinking their blades into it.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 more damage.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

Name: Deino
Flavor-Text: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Since their two heads do not get along and compete with each other for food, they always eat too much.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: All Energy attached to this Pokémon are [D] Energy instead of their usual type.
Effects: Does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

Name: Escavalier
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon evolve by wearing the shell covering of a Shelmet. The steel armor protects their whole body.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: Blades comprise this Pokémon’s entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: Attach a [M] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Does 20 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

Name: Durant
Effects: For each of your Durant in play, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves against Heatmor.

Name: Cobalion
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has a body and heart of steel. Its glare is sufficient to make even an unruly Pokémon obey it.

Name: Audino
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It touches others with the feelers on its ears, using the sound of their heartbeats to tell how they are feeling.

Name: Axew
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

Name: Fraxure
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they’ve been fighting.

Name: Haxorus
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel.

Name: Druddigon
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It races through narrow caves, using its sharp claws to catch prey. The skin on its face is harder than a rock.

Name: Cover Fossil
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Tirtouga you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Eviolite
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is a Basic Pokémon, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: N
Description: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a card for each of his or her remaining Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Plume Fossil
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Archen you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rocky Helmet
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Super Rod
Description: Shuffle 3 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Xtransceiver
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Virizion
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Leaf Wallop attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its head sprouts horns as sharp as blades. Using whirlwind-like movements, it confounds and swiftly cuts opponents.

Name: Victini
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack, you may ignore all effects of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. You can’t use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.
Effects: Move all Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Terrakion
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its charge is strong enough to break through a giant castle wall in one blow. This Pokémon is spoken of in legends.

Name: Cobalion
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has a body and heart of steel. Its glare is sufficient to make even an unruly Pokémon obey it.

Name: N
Description: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a card for each of his or her remaining Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: This is an extremely rare Meowth card. Maybe good fortune will come to you if you hold this card!
